incentive_impact.1: Logged on record. No impact on monthly incentives.
incentive_impact.2: 50% incentive deduction for the month of violation.
incentive_impact.3: Employee is ineligible for any incentives during the 90-day monitoring period.
incentive_impact.4: Employee is ineligible for incentives during the 120-day monitoring period and ineligible for the next quarterly bonus.
incentive_impact.5: Employee is ineligible for incentives for 180 days and ineligible for promotion consideration for one (1) full year from the date of this notice.
incentive_impact.6: All pending and future incentives are forfeited. Employee is referred to HR for a termination review interview.

probationary_consequences.1: Violation logged. No change to probationary status.
probationary_consequences.2: Violation logged. Probationary supervisor must conduct a formal coaching session within 7 days.
probationary_consequences.3: Employee is flagged for non-regularization review. Regularization will be reassessed at the end of probation.
probationary_consequences.4: Employee receives a no-recommendation for regularization. Contract review required before probation ends.
probationary_consequences.5: Probationary contract is subject to immediate review by HR. Continued employment is not guaranteed.
probationary_consequences.6: Probationary contract is terminated, subject to HR interview and due process.

violations.description: Covers tardiness, extended or unauthorized breaks, no-shows, undertime, and failure to follow attendance protocols.
examples: Repeated tardiness (3+ instances in a month)
Extended lunch breaks beyond allowed time
No-show without prior notice
Leaving the workstation without proper logging
Failure to clock in or out

severity_guidelines.1: First or second isolated tardiness; minor break overrun
severity_guidelines.2: Pattern of tardiness within a single month; repeated extended breaks
severity_guidelines.3: No-show without notice; significant pattern of attendance issues
severity_guidelines.4: Multiple no-shows; chronic attendance violations after prior warnings
severity_guidelines.5: Job abandonment (3+ consecutive no-shows); attendance fraud

default_consequences: Issuance of this Notice to Explain (NTE)
Mandatory written response within 5 calendar days
Monitoring period as indicated by severity level
Incentive impact as defined by severity level
Possible disciplinary action upon repeat offense

violations.description: Covers failure to meet performance targets, low productivity, idle time, and failure to follow standard operating procedures (SOPs) that result in poor work output.
examples: Failure to meet monthly productivity quotas
Excessive idle time during shift
Repeated errors in deliverables
Failure to complete assigned tasks within deadlines
Negligence resulting in rework or customer complaints

severity_guidelines.1: First-time minor underperformance; isolated missed task
severity_guidelines.2: Repeated minor underperformance within a single review period
severity_guidelines.3: Consistent failure to meet quotas; placement on Performance Improvement Plan (PIP)
severity_guidelines.4: Failure to improve following a PIP; significant productivity loss
severity_guidelines.5: Gross negligence resulting in financial loss or major service disruption

default_consequences: Issuance of this Notice to Explain (NTE)
Mandatory written response within 5 calendar days
Performance Improvement Plan (PIP) where applicable
Monitoring period as indicated by severity level
Incentive impact as defined by severity level

violations.description: Covers violations of company policy that do not directly involve misconduct or fraud — such as dress code, unauthorized use of company equipment, or breach of standard operating procedures.
examples: Dress code violations
Unauthorized use of company equipment or property
Use of personal devices during prohibited hours
Breach of documented SOPs (non-safety-critical)
Failure to comply with company communications protocol

severity_guidelines.1: First-time minor dress code or protocol violation
severity_guidelines.2: Repeated minor violations; unauthorized equipment use
severity_guidelines.3: Deliberate breach of SOP; repeated policy violations after warning
severity_guidelines.4: Serious policy violation causing operational disruption or risk

default_consequences: Issuance of this Notice to Explain (NTE)
Mandatory written response within 5 calendar days
Mandatory refresher on company policy
Monitoring period as indicated by severity level
Incentive impact as defined by severity level

violations.description: Covers behavioral issues including insubordination, disrespect toward colleagues or supervisors, workplace conflict, and unprofessional conduct.
examples: Insubordination — refusal to follow reasonable instructions
Disrespectful behavior toward coworkers or supervisors
Engaging in verbal conflict during work hours
Use of inappropriate language
Repeated unprofessional conduct after coaching

severity_guidelines.3: Repeated incidents; verbal conflict with coworkers
severity_guidelines.4: Insubordination toward direct supervisor; pattern of disruptive behavior
severity_guidelines.5: Aggressive or threatening behavior; behavior causing significant team disruption

default_consequences: Issuance of this Notice to Explain (NTE)
Mandatory written response within 5 calendar days
Mandatory behavioral coaching or mediation
Monitoring period as indicated by severity level
Incentive impact as defined by severity level
Possible suspension pending investigation for higher severity

violations.description: Covers verbal harassment, sexual harassment, discriminatory behavior, bullying, and any conduct that creates a hostile work environment. All cases trigger immediate formal investigation.
examples: Verbal harassment of a coworker
Sexual harassment (verbal, physical, or digital)
Discriminatory remarks based on protected characteristics
Workplace bullying or intimidation
Retaliation against a complainant

severity_guidelines.3: Confirmed verbal harassment of a non-severe nature (single incident)
severity_guidelines.5: Sexual harassment; severe bullying; retaliation against complainant
severity_guidelines.6: Severe harassment with documented evidence; gross misconduct — termination review

default_consequences: Immediate issuance of this Notice to Explain (NTE)
Mandatory written response within 3 calendar days (expedited)
Immediate investigation by HR and Ethics Committee
Possible immediate suspension pending investigation
Mandatory sensitivity and ethics training (if employment continues)
Monitoring period as indicated by severity level
Incentive impact as defined by severity level
Termination review for Level 6 cases

violations.description: Covers acts of dishonesty including theft, data falsification, time theft (e.g., buddy punching), confidentiality breaches, and any act that compromises the integrity of the company or its employees.
examples: Falsification of attendance, timesheets, or performance records
Theft of company property, funds, or intellectual property
Buddy punching or clocking in/out for another employee
Unauthorized disclosure of confidential information
Misrepresentation in official company documents
Conflict of interest not disclosed to management

severity_guidelines.4: Minor data falsification; first-time time theft (small magnitude)
severity_guidelines.5: Significant data falsification; theft of company property; confidentiality breach
severity_guidelines.6: Major fraud causing financial loss; severe breach of trust — termination review

default_consequences: Immediate issuance of this Notice to Explain (NTE)
Mandatory written response within 3 calendar days (expedited)
Immediate suspension pending investigation
Investigation by HR, Finance (if applicable), and Legal
Monitoring period as indicated by severity level
Incentive impact as defined by severity level
Possible legal action and recovery of damages
Termination review for Level 6 cases
